title: History, cycles and limits

publication.changeSummary: Source-linked AI Foundations draft for History, cycles and limits; all claims, examples, metrics, code, safety boundaries, and answer oracles require independent human review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Artificial Intelligence; August 2023; AI Knowledge Area: fundamental issues, intelligent agents, history, and societal context; atomic topic: History, cycles and limits
sources.claim: AI has developed through interacting symbolic, search, probabilistic, statistical, and neural traditions, with progress and setbacks shaped by compute, data, evaluation, funding, and gaps between demos and robust capability.

canonicalExplanation.summary: AI has developed through interacting symbolic, search, probabilistic, statistical, and neural traditions, with progress and setbacks shaped by compute, data, evaluation, funding, and gaps between demos and robust capability.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A modern system combines neural representations, search, hand-authored constraints, and probabilistic scoring rather than belonging to one pure historical camp. Your goal is to place major approach families in context and identify an overgeneralized historical claim. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: pf problem io; python quality project; AI, automation and intelligence claims. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Bounded operational model
sections.body: AI has developed through interacting symbolic, search, probabilistic, statistical, and neural traditions, with progress and setbacks shaped by compute, data, evaluation, funding, and gaps between demos and robust capability. Any capability or quality claim remains limited to an explicit task, population, conditions, baseline, and recorded evidence.

sections.heading: Failure boundary and non-claim
sections.body: Historical periods overlap, methods combine, labels shift, benchmark gains can coexist with brittle transfer, and simplified boom-winter narratives omit institutions, labor, harms, and prior work. The learner must reject this overclaim: AI history is a straight sequence in which neural networks replaced symbolic and statistical methods and each benchmark record proves general progress.

includes: Recognize symbolic, statistical and neural traditions and recurring gaps between benchmark and general capability.
Place major approach families in context and identify an overgeneralized historical claim.
excludes: Historical periods overlap, methods combine, labels shift, benchmark gains can coexist with brittle transfer, and simplified boom-winter narratives omit institutions, labor, harms, and prior work.

outcomes: Place major approach families in context and identify an overgeneralized historical claim.

examples.title: History, cycles and limits: bounded direct case
examples.situation: A modern system combines neural representations, search, hand-authored constraints, and probabilistic scoring rather than belonging to one pure historical camp.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the exact data, state, metric, or tool behavior: A modern system combines neural representations, search, hand-authored constraints, and probabilistic scoring rather than belonging to one pure historical camp.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.

Step 3 — Verify the claim boundary with: Place claims in overlapping technical and social context, identify the evaluation boundary, and distinguish benchmark improvement from general or deployment capability.
examples.result: This example supports only the atomic outcome because Place claims in overlapping technical and social context, identify the evaluation boundary, and distinguish benchmark improvement from general or deployment capability.

examples.title: History, cycles and limits: stress and transfer case
examples.situation: A benchmark record is announced as a solved domain, but changed data and real workflows expose distribution, cost, and safety failures.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name the changed population, input, assumption, or failure: A benchmark record is announced as a solved domain, but changed data and real workflows expose distribution, cost, and safety failures.

Step 2 — Keep the non-claim visible: Historical periods overlap, methods combine, labels shift, benchmark gains can coexist with brittle transfer, and simplified boom-winter narratives omit institutions, labor, harms, and prior work.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting overclaim: AI history is a straight sequence in which neural networks replaced symbolic and statistical methods and each benchmark record proves general progress.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Place major approach families in context and identify an overgeneralized historical claim.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: AI history is a straight sequence in which neural networks replaced symbolic and statistical methods and each benchmark record proves general progress.
misconceptions.correction: Place claims in overlapping technical and social context, identify the evaluation boundary, and distinguish benchmark improvement from general or deployment capability.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Identify the first unsupported inference in this belief and the evidence required to narrow it: AI history is a straight sequence in which neural networks replaced symbolic and statistical methods and each benchmark record proves general progress.

analogy.example: a map that labels what an automated system observes, claims, chooses, and cannot establish can introduce History, cycles and limits only after its canonical technical definition and evidence boundary are stated.
usefulFor: separating techniques, environments, agents, evidence, and historical limits
limitations: maps simplify contested intelligence concepts and cannot turn benchmark evidence into general capability
